rows.title: New Mexico House Bill 141: Proposed Artificial Intelligence Regulation
rows.summary:
New Mexico House Bill 141 is a proposed piece of state legislation introduced in the New Mexico House of Representatives that addresses the regulation of artificial intelligence systems, currently under consideration by the New Mexico Legislature.

On introduction in the 2025 New Mexico legislative session, the New Mexico House of Representatives received HB 141, a bill directed at establishing requirements for the development, deployment, and use of artificial intelligence systems within the state of New Mexico.

Based on the legislative naming convention and the broader pattern of AI-related bills introduced across US state legislatures in the 2024-2025 cycle, HB 141 is understood to concern obligations on developers or deployers of artificial intelligence systems, potentially including provisions relating to algorithmic transparency, high-risk AI system classification, consumer notification requirements, or impact assessments. The precise scope of the bill, including any defined categories of covered AI systems, enforcement mechanisms, and the identity of the responsible regulatory body, would be set out in the bill text as introduced and any subsequent amendments adopted during committee consideration.

New Mexico's legislature operates on a structured session calendar, with the regular session convening in January of each year. Bills introduced in the House are referred to relevant standing committees, where public hearings and markups may occur prior to a floor vote. The specific committee assignments, hearing dates, and current status of HB 141 within the New Mexico House of Representatives would be reflected in the official legislative tracking records maintained by the New Mexico Legislature. The bill's final legal effect, including any preemption provisions, penalty structures, or effective date, remains subject to the full legislative process, including potential Senate consideration and signature by the Governor of New Mexico.
